content: Opened Slack before even brushing because I remembered the Friday message from Vikram saying we should revisit the numbers. Revisit is such a harmless word but my stomach took it as you have messed up. By 9:20 I had already made a new tracker, changed the deck colors and replied to four things nobody asked me to reply on. The actual forecast, the one thing which can decide if this launch looks sensible, I kept for after lunch because I wanted a clean two hours. Clean two hours never came. I said yes to helping Rhea with her client note and then felt irritated that people always come to me. Truth is I like being the person they come to. It makes me feel useful and also gives me an excuse if my own work is late. Logged off at 10:47, ordered dinner, watched half an episode while checking Teams. Tomorrow no random helping, first forecast. I mean it this time.

The review went better than expected. Vikram said good recovery and moved to the next slide, maybe six seconds total, but I replayed those words through the afternoon. I sent the deck on the group even though everyone was already in the meeting. Wanted it to be visible, I guess. Then Mehul asked one question about the source data and suddenly the whole good feeling disappeared. I spent forty minutes drafting a reply so it would not sound defensive, deleted it, called him instead, and he was genuinely just asking. Why do I hear accusation everywhere? Did not start the pricing note. Again. I did reorganize the project folder and clear 312 emails, which felt productive until I looked at the time. Came home with energy because of the praise and told myself I should ask for the promotion this cycle. Five minutes later I opened LinkedIn and saw somebody younger becoming Director. Closed it. Opened it again.

Blocked 8 to 10 for deep work and spent the first twenty minutes finding the right focus playlist. Then coffee. Then I thought I should first read the old research so I don't make a basic mistake. At 10:15 the calendar started. The pricing note is not even difficult, it is just the kind of work where my thinking will be visible and somebody can say the thinking is weak. Much easier to improve a spreadsheet that already exists. I stayed very busy the whole day and still carried the same task home. Told Ananya I cannot meet because work is mad this week. Work is always mad when I am avoiding one specific thing. She said okay, but the short okay. I could have gone for dinner. Instead I sat with the laptop open, scrolled on my phone, and felt guilty in two directions. At 12:30 I wrote the first ugly paragraph. It was fine. Annoying how often the wall is only before starting.

News came that Karan got promoted. I congratulated him immediately and actually I am happy for him, but my body became hot when everyone started adding well deserved. We joined almost together. He speaks early in meetings even when the idea is half cooked. I wait till mine is complete and then the conversation has moved on. I call it being thoughtful, maybe it is fear. Updated two lines of my CV during lunch. Then I looked at job descriptions and each one had something I have not done, enterprise pricing, SQL, team management of five plus. Saved twelve roles and applied to zero. If I apply and they reject me, I will have proof that I am behind. If I don't apply I can keep believing I am underpaid but capable. This is not a clever strategy. Stayed late to finish the note and secretly hoped Vikram would notice the timestamp. He probably did not.

Skipped gym because sleep is also health, then used most of the extra twenty minutes checking mail. So neither sleep nor health. Friday was calls, calls. I volunteered to take the action tracker because nobody spoke and now I own eleven follow-ups from a meeting I did not even need to run. Vikram left at 6 and said don't work late. I stayed till 8:40, mostly fixing formatting. Bought tiramisu on the way back because week over. Ate the full thing and then felt bad about sugar. Why can't one dessert remain one dessert.

Papa asked how work is and I said very good, a lot of responsibility now. I did not say I spend half the week afraid someone will discover I am improvising. He started talking about a cousin buying a flat and sent me a loan calculator. I know he means security. I also know the salary looks large to him, so changing jobs without another offer feels irresponsible. I checked my bank app after the call, then the rent, SIP, insurance, money I send home, and felt trapped and lucky together. Ananya wanted to go out but I said I need one quiet day. By evening I was lonely and a little angry that nobody had made a plan for me. Ridiculous because I rejected the plan. Opened the office laptop at 7 just to reduce Monday anxiety. Two hours disappeared. Nothing was urgent. I keep borrowing from the weekend to make Monday less scary and Monday is still scary.

Made a proper reset today. New Notion page with work, health, money, relationships, learning. Color coded the habits and put morning walk at 6:30, reading at 7, office by 9. Also ordered a notebook because maybe writing by hand will make me consistent. This is notebook number four, actually five if the small black one counts. I spent nearly three hours designing a week I have no energy to live. Laundry still on chair. The blue shirt has been there since Thursday and I keep moving it to the bed at night and back to chair in morning. Watched a Sunday reset video while eating chips. The woman had glass jars for lentils and somehow this made me feel behind in life. Called Ma late and kept saying hmm while reading an article about becoming a strategic leader. She asked twice if I was listening. Felt bad after and added call parents properly to the tracker. Missing the point probably. Sunday evening tight chest came around 7:30. Checked Monday calendar four times. I want work under control before I rest, but work is never fully under control, so no surprise there. Sleep by 11 today. Need to iron something also. It is 12:18 while writing this.

Vikram said I need to operate at the next level and be less execution focused. I nodded like it was clear. What does next level even mean? After the meeting I took on the launch dashboard, the client summary and the weekly review because that looked like ownership. By evening I was doing more execution than last week. I should have asked him for examples but I wanted to appear like I understood. The fear of looking confused keeps me confused for longer. I asked two colleagues what they think he meant and got two different answers. Spent the rest of the day watching how senior people speak, when they interrupt, what goes in their updates. Almost no attention left for my actual judgment. Ananya asked if I booked the doctor for the headaches. I said tomorrow. I have said tomorrow for three weeks. Somehow I can coordinate five people at work and cannot make one phone call for myself.

Rhea messaged can you quickly look at this and I knew quickly would not be quick. Still said sure. Her document had no clear structure, so I rewrote most of it instead of leaving comments. She thanked me and presented it in the leadership call. They liked it. Nobody knew I had touched it. I felt used, which is unfair because I offered more than she asked for and never told her what I needed. My own dashboard was late, so I skipped lunch and became short with the analyst when one number did not match. Apologized later. The pattern is embarrassingly simple: say yes to be liked, hide the cost, then punish somebody safer. Came home thinking I need stronger boundaries. Watched three videos about saying no. Tomorrow if anyone asks, I will say I am at capacity. Unless it is important. Or someone senior. Or genuinely quick. So basically the same rule as today.

Finally got three uninterrupted hours and found the error in our conversion logic. It changes the forecast in a useful way and I felt properly awake for the first time in days. No meetings, no reactions, just brain working. Then a weird emptiness because nobody saw those three hours. I posted the finding in the channel with a longer explanation than needed and checked for replies every few minutes. Two thumbs up, one nice catch. I know the work matters even without applause, but apparently my nervous system did not receive that memo. In the afternoon I joined a meeting where I had nothing to add because the leadership team was there. Said one point already written in the pre-read. Felt visible but also fake. The useful work and the visible work are not always same, and I keep choosing visibility when I am insecure, then complain I have no time to think. Need to remember today's morning. Quiet work gave more energy than the whole week of being available.

Canceled dinner with Ananya again because the client moved the review. She said work always gets the version of me that can adjust and she gets whatever is left. I immediately explained that this month is unusual. She laughed, not in a funny way, and listed March, April, May. I wanted her to understand the pressure instead of hearing what she was saying. During the client call my section lasted seven minutes. I could have joined dinner late. Instead I stayed online because leaving right after my part might look bad. Nobody asked me to stay. At 10:30 I sent Ananya a long sorry message and felt I had done something. Words are easier than changing the calendar. I keep telling people I am doing this for a better future, promotion, savings, more freedom. But the future person receiving all these benefits might have nobody waiting for dinner. That thought scared me, then a Slack notification came and I opened it.

Salary came. Felt rich for maybe ten minutes, then rent, SIP, money home, card bill. Looked at the flat calculator Papa sent and spent lunch browsing apartments I don't want to buy yet. Owning something looks like progress in a screenshot. Work was unusually calm. I found next month's plan and started cleaning it, no one asked. If nobody needs me I become restless. Booked the doctor finally, then moved it because of weekly review. Review can happen without me. Did not enjoy typing that.

Slept till 10:40 and woke guilty, as if Saturday had a performance rating. The body clearly wanted sleep but my mind called it wasting the best hours. Made coffee, opened LinkedIn and saw one person announcing an MBA, another joining Google, somebody building a startup on weekends. By noon my normal life looked embarrassingly small. I researched executive courses for two hours. Added three to a spreadsheet with fees and deadlines. I have two unfinished courses already, one bought during the last career panic. Did not open either. Went to the grocery store and felt better choosing tomatoes, maybe because there is no personal brand around tomatoes. Ananya came over in evening. I tried not to touch the phone but kept seeing it light up. Nothing important. My attention has become like a door without a lock, anyone can enter. We watched a stupid movie and I laughed properly. Then felt pressure to make Sunday productive enough to compensate.

Ma video called and said my face looks tired. I told her bad lighting. She asked if I eat fruit and I got irritated because I am thirty and can manage food. After the call I realized lunch was coffee and two biscuits. Cleaned the house aggressively, meal prepped, put gym clothes out, decided this week will be balanced. I always attack exhaustion with a stricter plan. By afternoon I was tired from preparing not to be tired. Read the first ten pages of a book on essentialism and highlighted almost every line, then accepted a Monday morning meeting during the time I blocked for the dashboard. The contradiction did not occur to me until now. I miss the version of me who could do something badly for fun. Everything now needs a reason, fitness, networking, skill building, quality time. Even rest has to improve Monday. Maybe this is why I don't feel rested. Anyway, alarm at 6. Gym must happen.

Missed the alarm, woke at 7:35 and immediately felt the week was spoiled. Skipped gym because forty minutes did not feel worth it if I could not do the full routine. This all or nothing thing wastes a lot of available forty minutes. At work I sent the old revenue number in the update. Mehul caught it within five minutes. I thanked him casually, corrected the message, then spent the next hour imagining Vikram forwarding the mistake somewhere. Nobody reacted. I became extra useful for the rest of the day, answered everything fast, made a detailed risk list, stayed late. Like I could repay one wrong number with enough visible labor. I did not tell anyone I was shaken because competent people do not get shaken by small corrections, according to the rule I invented. Headache was back by 6. Doctor appointment is now Thursday. Do not move it again.

A new person asked a very basic question in the team call and did not look embarrassed at all. Everyone answered normally. I was almost jealous. I spend so much energy making sure my questions sound advanced that sometimes I do not ask and then work around the gap for days. Later I needed finance to explain one assumption. Drafted the message, added my current understanding to show I had done homework, added two possible answers, made it so long they called me. The answer took thirty seconds. Had coffee with Karan. He said he asked for promotion directly in January and kept asking what was missing. I had made a story that his promotion came because he is naturally confident. Maybe confidence is partly just tolerating an awkward conversation. I want to ask in my one to one tomorrow. Properly ask, without first listing all the reasons they may say no. Practiced the sentence while walking home. It sounded greedy in my mouth, which is interesting.

Had the one to one. I said I would love to understand how growth might look over the next few cycles. So many soft words. What I meant was am I getting promoted or not. Vikram said dependable, strong executor, need to show more strategic ownership. Same answer in a new order. I smiled and said makes sense, yes absolutely. Did not ask what evidence he needs or which cycle. I had practiced the direct sentence yesterday and still did not say it. Came out angry at him for not answering what I did not ask. Went to washroom, looked at myself and actually said coward, which is dramatic. Then in the planning call I volunteered to lead cross team coordination because ownership. Calendar now has nine extra meetings, two at 8:30 because US team. I think I may be becoming better at the wrong job, collecting everyone's updates while losing time to have my own view. Or maybe I am making excuses because strategic work is harder to measure. Ananya noticed mood in two minutes. I said just tired. She asked what happened, I said nothing, then felt she was not supporting me enough. This is same nonsense outside also. Want something, hide it, become hurt that person did not locate it with radar. Had maggi for dinner. Too salty but finished.

Planning started and three people asked where the template is. I made a very nice template last night without deciding the two questions it should answer. Everyone filled it differently. More cleanup for me. Went to doctor finally. Sleep, screen, stress, obviously. He asked hours and I lied, said six to seven. Why lie to doctor? Bought medicines, answered office messages from cab though Vikram wrote pick tomorrow. Kept phone outside bedroom at night. Went and checked after fifteen minutes because maybe an emergency had developed. It had not.

Vikram sent a message at 11:52 saying can we talk tomorrow about the plan. I saw only can we talk and my heart started. Read the deck again in bed, found five possible problems, wrote answers in Notes. Slept badly. In the morning he wanted to move the review to Tuesday because his flight changed. That was all. I lost half a night to punctuation and hierarchy. The annoying part is I know this pattern and still believe each new message is the one where trouble is real. Was foggy all day, so I avoided the analytical work and did easy follow-ups. Then felt behind and stayed late. Fear ruins sleep, less sleep ruins focus, poor focus creates delay, delay gives fear actual material. A neat little machine. I wrote that down because it is probably the main thing costing me. But knowing the machine is not same as switching it off. Tonight muted Slack after 9. Checked through browser at 9:26, loophole found.

Met college friends for brunch after almost eight months of saying we should meet soon. Nikhil is moving to Singapore, Sana has a baby who already looks like her father, Deep bought a flat near Noida and spent twenty minutes explaining interest rates. We laughed a lot. I was also keeping score in my head. Salary, title, marriage, city. Felt like a bad friend and continued doing it. When they asked what is new with me I talked too much about the launch because work is the cleanest proof that something is happening. Nobody asked my title. I brought up that I am leading planning now. The food was good, especially the chilli eggs, but when bill came I noticed who put which card down, again the stupid score. Came home and searched Singapore salaries. Then home loans. Then whether it is too late to do an MBA at thirty. Then fertility timelines even though Ananya and I have not decided marriage. My browser history looks like five different people panicking. She asked in evening if I see marriage with her. I said obviously yes but this year is complicated. She asked what changes next year. I said promotion cycle, savings, maybe role change. She said those are not answers. I know. We sat quietly and I could hear neighbour's pressure cooker. Opened Swiggy just to do something with hands. Ordered too much.

Started the strategy course I bought in February. Watched at 1.5 speed while replying to messages, finished two modules and remember nearly nothing. Still marked them complete. Completion has become a visual thing, green ticks, inbox zero, closed Jira tickets. Understanding is slower and does not give a satisfying notification. Made notes about thinking from first principles, then copied the template into Notion and spent time making it look clean. The real work would be writing what I think our product should stop doing. I have opinions but putting them down means people can disagree. So I collect more frameworks. It feels like preparation and protects me from being tested. Afternoon nap became two hours. Woke anxious and made a list of nine things for Monday. Ananya did not call. I wanted to call her but also wanted her to call first, some childish proof that she still wants this. Finally sent a meme. Very brave communication.

In leadership review, Karan presented the conversion issue I had found. He said the team found it, which is technically true, but I wanted my name there. I waited for a natural opening to add context. No opening came. Afterwards I told myself secure people don't need credit, then complained to Rhea for twenty minutes. She asked why I did not speak. I said I didn't want to make it awkward. Apparently I prefer private resentment to public awkwardness. Karan later messaged me nice work on the analysis. He may not even know I expected a mention. I drafted a calm note to Vikram about my contribution, then deleted it because it looked needy. Spent evening updating the analysis with even more detail so the next version would be impossible to overlook. This is the loop again: stay quiet, feel invisible, work harder, still not practice speaking. Hard work is respectable so it hides the fear nicely.

Applied for one job today, almost. Expected salary field led to six tabs. Then I found a possible LinkedIn connection for referral, drafted a message, did not send because we spoke once in 2022. Application timed out. Saved the role again, classic. At night I reopened it and submitted without referral. Felt slightly sick after pressing send. I keep saying I want options and then avoid doing the one thing that creates an option. Current job is tiring but at least I know where all the bodies are buried. New place may find out I am only good here. Anyway one application sent.

Dinner with Ananya. I kept the phone face down but touched it every time the conversation paused. She asked if I even enjoy my work. I gave a ten minute answer about growth, market, learning, team quality. So probably I don't know. There are moments I enjoy, finding the error, making sense of a mess, helping someone think. Most days I am managing how I am perceived while doing the job. That second job is taking more energy than the first. She said she does not need a five year plan tonight, only wants me to be in the room. I got defensive because it sounded too easy and I have trained myself to respect difficult solutions. We walked home without fixing everything. I did not check Slack for forty minutes. Nothing collapsed. When I finally checked, there were seven messages and all could wait. This should feel like evidence, but my brain treats it like I got lucky once.

Three coffees, no proper lunch, then wondered why my hands were shaky in the 4 p.m. call. I wear busyness like it proves demand. Ate at desk even though nobody else was there. The planning project is now behind because I keep agreeing to custom requests from every team. If I make one decision, somebody may dislike it. If I keep gathering inputs, delay looks collaborative. That is a hard sentence to admit. Vikram asked when he can see the recommendation and I gave Friday, then instantly regretted it. Stayed late but this time wrote the ugly version before fixing slides. It has an actual point. Maybe not the perfect point, but one that can be argued with. Left office at 8, earlier than usual lately, and felt almost rebellious. On the cab ride I imagined the feedback and rewrote half of it in my head. I can leave the laptop, apparently not the meeting.

Presented the recommendation. There were questions, one disagreement, then a decision. No disaster. Vikram said this is the kind of point of view he wants more from me. I should feel proud. I did for maybe twenty minutes. Then I started thinking I should have quantified the second risk and whether Karan's question made me look unprepared. The finish line moves so fast it is almost comic. Team went for drinks and I nearly skipped because I had follow-ups, but went. People talked about pets, rent, bad dates, parents, normal life. I realized I know their project status better than I know them. Got home a little late and did not open laptop. Kept reaching toward where it normally sits. The application from Tuesday sent an assessment invite. First feeling excitement, second feeling I need two weeks to prepare, third feeling maybe withdraw before they see my gaps. I have until Wednesday. Do not turn this into another course collection project.

Ma's birthday. Cake reached twenty minutes late and had Happy Birthday Maa written as MAA in huge letters, she loved it. Transferred money for dinner, called all efficient. During video call I was reading assessment instructions in another tab. She told me which relatives came and I asked the same question twice. She noticed and said you are working, call later. I said no no, tell me, while still looking at laptop. Papa took phone around the room. Masi's hair is almost fully grey now. Chotu is taller. The drawing room curtain has changed. Felt suddenly like home is moving without me. Wanted to book tickets immediately, then checked July calendar for the least damaging weekend. Wrote three options in Notes, compared flight prices, did not book. Ananya came and helped with assessment case. I corrected her approach twice, as if she is my analyst and not someone giving Saturday for me. She went quiet. I apologized, ordered Ma's favorite chilli paneer for us and we called again during cake cutting. Screen froze exactly when Ma blew candle. We laughed a lot at the frozen face. I still checked assessment after Ananya slept.

Saw the full month in my head today. Say yes, lose focus, stay late, sleep badly, make small mistake, work harder to prove I am not the mistake, cancel life, feel lonely, then build new system on Sunday. Repeat. Another one is wanting something but not asking. Promotion, credit, time with Ananya, even help. I make the want very polite until it is invisible, then feel nobody sees me. Wrote all this in the new notebook. The page looks like a mad flowchart with arrows going everywhere. Had the usual urge to fix all of it tomorrow. Wake 5, gym, no sugar, deep work, direct promotion talk, assessment, call parents, meal prep. Actually wrote 5 AM in large letters and boxed it. Then remembered every failed Monday from this month. Crossed most things out. Keeping two, sleep before 11 and finish assessment draft before Slack. Feels too small and honestly not impressive. Maybe that is okay. The rest of Sunday was nothing major. Bought vegetables, forgot coriander. Watched highlights from a match I already knew the result of. Ananya sent only good night, no heart. I spent ten minutes interpreting it and then called. She was sleepy. We spoke normally. Phone is in kitchen now. Came back twice to confirm alarm is on. It is.

Woke at 7 after nearly seven hours and still called myself lazy for a second. Did assessment for fifty minutes before Slack. At work Rhea asked for help. Said I can review twenty minutes tomorrow but can't rewrite today. Felt rude. She said sure thanks and moved on. Left by 6:40, called Ma from cab, heard the birthday story properly. Normal day. Kept waiting for guilt or crisis to arrive.

Submitted the assessment at 11:43, seventeen minutes before my personal deadline and one full day before theirs. Immediately noticed a weak sentence and wanted to email a revised file. Did not. In the one to one I asked Vikram directly which promotion cycle we are discussing and what two outcomes he needs to see. My face was hot, but I asked. He said September is possible if I lead the pricing decision and show the planning result. Clear enough to work with, also clear enough that the answer may still be no. Strange relief. I told Ananya and she asked if September means our life waits until September. Fair question. Booked tickets home for the second weekend of July before checking every possible meeting. I am not suddenly balanced. Checked Slack during dinner, compared my assessment to imaginary candidates, and almost bought another interview course. But I can see the loops a little earlier now. Sometimes early enough to choose differently, sometimes only while writing this.
